LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. THE WITNESSES ARE SASB EMPLOYEES WHOSE TESTIMONY APPEARS REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF MAY OBTAIN DEPOSITIONS OF WITNESSES IN THE ORDER PLAINTIFF SEES FIT. SASB SHALL PRODUCE DOUGLAS PARK, JEROME LAVIGNE-DELVILLE, AMANDA MEDRESS, DEB MARTIN, ELI REISMAN, AND HARRISON THOMAS FOR THEIR DEPOSITIONS ON DATES WITHIN 20 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THE ORDER (COUNSEL SHALL MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE SEQUENCE OF WITNESSES TO BE DEPOSED). SAID DATES TO BE MUTUALLY AGREED UPON BETWEEN THE PARTIES' COUNSEL. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF FROM DEFENSE COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,060.00 (TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-24-2016 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-11-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-07-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MARK C. LE CLERC of Le Clerc & Le Clerc, LLP, Tel No. (415) 445-0900 appearing for Plaintiff Rebecca Harris;
Atty. SARAH R. NICHOLS of Ogletree, Deakins, Nash, Smoak & Stewart, P.C., Tel No. (415) 442-4810 appearing for Defendant Sustainability Accounting Standards Board.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF REBECCA HARRIS' Motion To Compel Depositions And Request For Monetary Sanctions is granted. The witnesses are SASB employees whose testimony appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Plaintiff may obtain depositions of witnesses in the order plaintiff sees fit. SASB shall produce Douglas Park, Jerome Lavigne-Delville, Amanda Medress, Deb Martin, Eli Reisman, and Harrison Thomas for their depositions on dates within 20 days from the date of the order (counsel shall meet and confer regarding the sequence of witnesses to be deposed). Said dates to be mutually agreed upon between the parties' counsel. Sanctions are awarded to plaintiff from defense counsel in the amount of $4,060.00 (to be paid within 30 days). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-26-2014 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Or Alternatively, Modify Subpoenas Served On Six Non Parties
Off Calendar Jan-26-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. CHRISTOPHER R. LECLERC of LE CLERC & LE CLERC, LLP, Tel No. (415) 445-0900 appearing for Plaintiff Rebecca Harris;
Atty. SARAH R. NICHOLS of Ogletree, Deakins, Nash, Smoak & Stewart, P.C., Tel No. (415) 442-4810 appearing for Defendant Sustainability Accounting Standards Board.

RULING - The Court required parties to appear. Matter argued by counsel. The Court ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF REBECCA HARRIS' Motion To Quash Or, Alternatively, Modify Subpoenas Served On Six Non Parties is denied. The Court is satisfied with the requested information provided by defense counsel at today's hearing. The defense may go forward on its four subpoenas regarding Plaintiff's former employers (which have been reviewed by the Court and deemed acceptable as currently drafted), its documents subpoena regarding Mr. Peterson, and its subpoena regarding Plaintiff's medical records. All the aforementioned subpoenas shall be subject to a protective order. DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/AJR)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions And Request For Monetary Sanctions

Appearances:
Christopher LeClerc, Esq. of Le Clerc & Le Clerc LLP, Tel. 415.445.0900, for plaintiff and moving party;
Sarah R. Nichols, Esq. of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewart PC, Tel. 415.536.3442, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: The parties did not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem. The matter was argued. After argument, PLAINTIFF REBECCA HARRIS' Motion To Compel Depositions And Request For Monetary Sanctions is continued to February 18, 2015 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. The Judge Pro Tem shall prepare a Report and Recommendation. Judge Pro Tem: David Helbraun; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

RULING: At the start of the hearing, the Court informed the parties of its tentative ruling. The matter was argued.
